text: Tanaka reads the drone feed at her command table.
The number sits on her screen and clean block type.
12,000 figures.
Each one broader than the widest soldier in her unit.
Each one carrying weapons forged from bone and black stone
that the thermal imaging cannot fully read.
The edges run cold where they should run hot.
Her agitant brings her the casualty projections.
She does not flinch.
She sets the tablet down and walks to the ridge edge.
The rift pulses in the distance
like a second heartbeat the earth never asked for.
Her soldiers dig trenches.
They anchor steel cable.
They load magazines with fingers that do not shake
because she taught them not to shake.
The Neanderthals have not advanced yet.
They are watching.
That is worse than an attack.
Watching means planning.
Planning means they understand what they are facing.
Tanaka orders the forward artillery
to load and sendier at mounts.
She says nothing else.
Her soldiers know what silence means.
